target.name: Vatn Systems
target.founder: Nelson Mills, Freddie Mills, Geoff Manchester

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $76M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $125M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: NODA AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $125M

data.narrative:
Vatn Systems is a Series A-stage defense and security company founded by Nelson Mills, Freddie Mills, and Geoff Manchester that has raised $76.5M to date. Valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 10 peers spanning the Defense & Security sector—2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including NODA AI, Anduril Industries, Helsing, Shield AI, and Saronic, predominantly at Series B stage.

Two triangulation methods prove applicable. Stage-progression analysis uses the $76.5M raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yielding $153M. Peer-median benchmarking across the 8-company private comp set yields a median valuation of $1.0B, with a range spanning $125M to $3.0B. The dispersion reflects heterogeneity in maturity and capability within the defense tech category.

These methods triangulate to a valuation range of $130M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $576M. The 8.5x spread between low and high estimates reflects meaningful uncertainty around Vatn's positioning relative to more mature Series B entrants. Key variables driving valuation within this range include customer diversification, technology differentiation versus the peer set, and capital efficiency relative to defense sector norms. Stage-progression implies a more conservative valuation; peer-median suggests higher comparable values, likely reflecting sector tailwinds and downstream demand.
